splits: Lock final test set before any tuning: 2023-01-01 to 2025-12-31
Use development period only for research: 2012-01-01 to 2022-12-31
Within development, run rolling walk-forward windows
Window template: 5 years train, 1 year validation, step forward 1 year
Example folds: train 2012-2016 validate 2017; train 2013-2017 validate 2018; train 2014-2018 validate 2019; train 2015-2019 validate 2020; train 2016-2020 validate 2021; train 2017-2021 validate 2022
Freeze specification after development-stage model selection
Run exactly one final evaluation on locked test set
No parameter changes after test evaluation
sweeps: Pre-register finite candidate set before running development tests
Disallow ad hoc parameter additions after sweep starts
multiple_testing_control: Use a White-style Reality Check over the full pre-registered candidate family
Define performance differential for each candidate versus benchmark net Sharpe or mean daily return after costs
Use stationary bootstrap on daily portfolio returns to preserve dependence
Bootstrap both candidate and benchmark series jointly
Compute null distribution of the maximum performance statistic across all tried candidates
Report family-wise adjusted p-value for best candidate
As robustness check, run SPA-style stepdown variant to reduce conservatism
If adjusted p-value > 0.05, treat apparent edge as unconfirmed
Count every materially evaluated variant in the candidate family, including abandoned variants if results were seen
overfitting_estimation: Use combinatorially symmetric cross-validation on the development period
For each candidate, evaluate ranks of in-sample versus out-of-sample performance across fold combinations
Estimate probability of backtest overfitting as fraction of trials where top in-sample candidates fall below median out-of-sample rank
Compute probability of loss out-of-sample for the selected candidate
Track rank correlation between in-sample and out-of-sample fold performance
Repeat estimation under baseline and stressed transaction cost assumptions
Flag strategy as overfit if estimated overfitting probability > 0.2
metrics: Primary: t-stat of mean daily return
Primary: family-wise adjusted p-value from White-style test
diagnostics: Long leg and short leg contribution separately
Exposure to size, value, momentum, quality, low-volatility factors
Stability of selected hyperparameters across folds
Skew, kurtosis, tail dependence, worst 5 days
Breadth: average number of active names and effective number of bets
Sensitivity to removing top 5 pnl names and top 5 pnl days
stop_rules: Stop iteration when locked test set has been touched once
Stop if White-style adjusted p-value remains > 0.05 after pre-registered sweep is exhausted
Stop if probability of backtest overfitting > 0.2 for selected candidate
Stop if out-of-sample Sharpe degrades by more than 25% under 1.5x cost stress
Stop if performance is driven by fewer than 2 years or fewer than 3 sectors
Stop if any change requires expanding the search space beyond the pre-registered family; start a new research cycle instead
Stop if no candidate meets minimum acceptance thresholds on development walk-forward median performance
Stop after one final frozen specification is chosen and evaluated on the locked test set
